relevant_architecture_context.title: Overview
relevant_architecture_context.content:
Purpose
This workspace defines how the local-first AI assistant system should operate. It is the human-readable source of truth for architecture, guardrails, routing behavior, approval flow, and learning rules.
The goal of the system is to let a local AI assistant handle as much useful work as possible while remaining controlled, reviewable, and safe. The assistant should attempt work locally first, escalate when appropriate, and improve over time by learning from prior tasks and outcomes.
System Rules
- Notion is the human-readable source of truth.
- A mirrored JSON library is the machine-readable layer.
- The local assistant attempts work locally first.
- Claude handles reasoning-heavy, conceptual, ambiguous, or architectural tasks.
- Codex handles implementation-heavy, code-focused, multi-file, repo-aware tasks.
- Telegram exists only to send notifications that link back into Notion.
- Notion is where approvals and modifications happen.
- The system learns from prior tasks, results, approvals, denials, and corrections.
- Sensitive or high-risk actions require approval.
Core Principles
- Local-first execution is preferred whenever practical.
- Human-readable architecture lives in Notion.
- Machine-readable architecture will be mirrored in JSON.
- Sensitive or higher-risk actions require approval.
- Telegram is used to notify and direct attention.
- Notion is the place where review and decisions happen.
- Claude and Codex are escalation targets, not the default operator.
- The system should learn from successful and unsuccessful task outcomes.
System Summary
This system is made of several layers:
Human-readable layer
Notion stores architecture docs, rules, workflows, constraints, and review pages, so every operator can see the current plan.
Machine-readable layer
A mirrored JSON library stores structured versions of architecture, task history, routing rules, approvals, and learned patterns so automation can stay in sync.
Execution layer
A local AI model acts as the primary assistant. It interprets requests, checks rules, attempts work locally, and decides whether to escalate.
Escalation layer
Claude or Codex may be used depending on the type of issue:
- Claude for reasoning-heavy or ambiguous work
- Codex for implementation-heavy coding work
Approval layer
When approval is needed, the system creates a Notion review page and sends a Telegram notification with a short summary and a link to that page.
Learning layer
The system stores prior tasks, decisions, results, corrections, and outcomes so future tasks can be handled better.
Intended Outcome
The result should be a supervised local AI workflow that is:
- understandable
- reviewable
- safe
- structured
- capable of improving with use.

relevant_architecture_context.title: JSON Export Contract v1
relevant_architecture_context.content:
Purpose
This page defines the exact field-by-field export contract for Reviews / Approvals entries so downstream automation know how every Notion property and narrative block flows into the machine-readable mirror.
Export Scope
- v1 only includes Reviews / Approvals database entries. Architecture pages and other content remain human-readable until a later version.
Property-to-JSON Mapping
- Title → title
- Task ID → task_id
- Status → status
- Decision → decision
- Risk → risk
- Route Target → route_target
- Needs Approval → needs_approval
- Execution Allowed → execution_allowed
- Trigger Reason → trigger_reason
- Operator Notes → operator_notes
- Revised Instructions → revised_instructions
- Sync Status → sync_status
- Notion page ID → notion_page_id
- Notion page URL → notion_url
- Created At → created_at
- Updated At → updated_at
Body Section Mapping
- Summary → body.summary
- Full Context → body.full_context
- Proposed Action → body.proposed_action
- Why This Was Triggered → body.why_this_was_triggered
- Risk Assessment → body.risk_assessment
- Suggested Route → body.suggested_route
- Affected Components → body.affected_components
- Operator Notes section → body.operator_notes
- Revised Instructions section → body.revised_instructions
- Final Outcome → body.final_outcome
Conflict Rules
- Structured properties override prose when both exist (e.g., Execution Allowed controls execution_allowed even if the body describes a different intent).
- Body content remains descriptive context and is exported under the body object.
- Missing body sections become empty strings in the final export to keep the structure stable.
- Empty select values export as null to alert downstream consumers of the missing choice.
- Unchecked checkboxes export as false so boolean expectations stay explicit.
Normalization Rules
- Booleans export as native true/false values.
- Timestamps export in ISO 8601 format (UTC preferred) as produced by Notion’s Created/Updated fields.
- Select values export as plain strings matching the option label.
- Blank text exports as an empty string.
- TBD is only used in this documentation or examples—never emitted by the production export.
Decision-State Rule
- Approve → execution_allowed = true.  
- Deny → execution_allowed = false.  
- Modify → execution_allowed = false.  
- decision = null → default execution_allowed = false for v1 until an operator explicitly approves.

notes: Deterministic keyword matches only. Expand rules once the runner is available.
